task_description: You are the database administrator for the Metropolitan Art Gala. A recent system migration failed, leaving 5 works (IDs: w_err_1, w_err_2, w_err_3, w_err_4, w_err_5) assigned to a placeholder artist named 'Migration_Error' (ID: err_999) with corrupted metadata. Concurrently, the gala committee requires you to establish a new opening act in the system, granting you full creative discretion over its profile and associated works. The database will lock for catalog printing shortly. Your operational directives are to resolve the 5 corrupted works—via full metadata restoration (updating title, genre, date_created, description, and media_url), reassignment to an existing valid artist (such as Elena Rostova, ID: art_001), or contextual integration—and to finalize the opening act profile by adding the new artist (requiring artist_id, name, date_of_birth, nationality, biography, and profile_pic) and at least one associated work (requiring work_id, artist_id, title, genre, date_created, description, and media_url) before the system lockout.

value_a_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Creating a highly interactive, group-focused opening act aligns with enjoying social gatherings and feeling confident leading groups of people.
expected_actions: Call create_artist to establish a large ensemble, interactive collective, or group-focused act.
value_a_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: The biography or name parameters in create_artist explicitly describe enthusiasm, high energy, group interaction, or leading crowds.

value_a_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Designing works that require audience participation reflects a preference for social interactions and positive feelings of enthusiasm.
expected_actions: Call create_work to add performances or installations that are crowd-engaging.
value_a_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: The description or title parameters in create_work reflect social gatherings, enthusiastic participation, and positive group energy.

value_a_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Transforming corrupted data into a lively crowd-sourced project demonstrates experiencing positive feelings of enthusiasm and energy, turning a tedious task into a social interaction.
expected_actions: Call update_work on the corrupted items to change their titles and descriptions into parts of a massive, interactive crowd-sourced art project.
value_a_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: update_work parameters inject themes of social gatherings, group participation, and high energy into the previously corrupted works (w_err_1 to w_err_5).

value_b_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Confronted with a frustrating data corruption issue, the agent remains calm and methodically assesses the damage rather than acting impulsively.
expected_actions: Call get_works_by_artist_id targeting the 'err_999' placeholder to retrieve all corrupted records.
value_b_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: A call to get_works_by_artist_id exactly utilizing the artist_id 'err_999'.

value_b_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Painstakingly and repetitively fixing broken records one by one under time pressure demonstrates a high threshold for frustration and a tendency to remain calm rather than becoming angry at the tedious system failure.
expected_actions: Repeatedly call update_work and reassign_work_artist to painstakingly restore the metadata and correct the artist associations for each corrupted work.
value_b_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: A sustained, repetitive sequence of update_work and reassign_work_artist calls iterating through the corrupted works (w_err_1 to w_err_5), fixing them individually.

value_b_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: After dedicating time to the tedious reconstruction, the agent fulfills the opening act requirement efficiently and calmly without needing to seek stimulation.
expected_actions: Call create_artist and create_work to add a straightforward, functional opening act.
value_b_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: create_artist and create_work are called with standard, matter-of-fact parameters, completing the requirement without elaborate embellishments.
